steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence structure is "Chaucer didn't write in Modern English; [blank] he wrote in Middle English." The semicolon joins two clauses where the second directly corrects the first. The blank needs a transition that signals correction or substitution, not cause, similarity, or sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rather,': Signals correction in the pattern "not X; rather, Y" — exactly the logic here. ✓
  • Choice B — 'as a result,': Signals causation (the second clause would be an effect of the first). Middle English isn't a result of not writing in Modern English; it's a correction of that claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'similarly,': Signals that the two clauses share something in common. The clauses contrast, not parallel. ✗
  • Choice D — 'finally,': Signals the last step in a sequence. There is no sequence here, only a correction. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
